tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show working tree status: current branch, staged changes, unstaged changes, and untracked files.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_log
tools.description: Show recent commit history.

count.description: Number of commits to show (e.g. 10). Defaults to 10 if omitted.

skip.description: Skip N commits before starting output (e.g. 10 to skip the first 10 commits). Use with count to paginate through history.

oneline.description: Use compact one-line format per commit

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

path.description: Limit log to commits affecting this file or directory path within the repository

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: Show differences between working tree, index, or commits.

staged.description: Show staged changes (index vs HEAD) instead of unstaged changes

ref.description: Compare against a specific commit, branch, or ref (e.g. 'main', 'HEAD~3')

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

path.description: Limit diff to a specific file or directory path within the repository

tools.name: git_diff_lines
tools.description: Read a range of lines from a git diff output with line numbers. Returns lines in the format {line_number}\t{content}. Use this when git_diff output is truncated to read the remaining lines without re-reading what was already shown. Set start_line to the line after the last shown line from the truncated output. When end_line is omitted, reads from start_line to the end of the output.

start_line.description: Line number to start reading at (1-indexed, inclusive)

end_line.description: Line number to end reading at (1-indexed, inclusive). When omitted, reads from start_line to the end of the output.

ref.description: Compare against a specific commit, branch, or ref

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

path.description: Limit diff to a specific file or directory path within the repository

tools.name: git_show
tools.description: Show the contents of a specific commit (message, author, diff).

ref.description: Commit hash, branch name, tag, or ref to show (e.g. 'HEAD', 'abc1234', 'main')

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_show_lines
tools.description: Read a range of lines from a git show output with line numbers. Returns lines in the format {line_number}\t{content}. Use this when git_show output is truncated to read the remaining lines without re-reading what was already shown. Set start_line to the line after the last shown line from the truncated output. When end_line is omitted, reads from start_line to the end of the output.

start_line.description: Line number to start reading at (1-indexed, inclusive)

end_line.description: Line number to end reading at (1-indexed, inclusive). When omitted, reads from start_line to the end of the output.

ref.description: Commit hash, branch name, tag, or ref to show

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: List branches. Shows all local branches with the current branch marked.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_add
tools.description: Stage files for the next commit.

paths.description: File path or pattern to stage (e.g. 'src/main.rs', '.')

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description: Create a new commit with staged changes.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_branch_create
tools.description: Create a new branch and switch to it.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Switch to an existing branch.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_branch_delete
tools.description: Delete a local branch. Use `force: true` to delete branches that were squash-merged (Git refuses `branch -d` for branches whose commit ancestry diverged from the target). Force-deleting a protected branch (`main`, `release/*`) is always blocked regardless of the `force` flag.

force.description: Force-delete the branch (use `git branch -D`). Required after squash merges where Git does not consider the branch fully merged. Protected branches (`main`, `release/*`) are always blocked. Default: false.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_merge
tools.description: Merge a branch into the current branch. Defaults to squash merge (--squash) which stages the combined diff without creating a merge commit — follow with git_commit to finalize.

squash.description: Squash the branch's commits into a single staged change (default: true)

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_rebase
tools.description: Rebase the current branch onto another branch or ref. Replays the current branch's commits on top of the target.

onto.description: The branch or ref to rebase onto (e.g. 'main', 'feat/my-feature')

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_cherry_pick
tools.description: Apply the changes introduced by specific commits onto the current branch. Defaults to creating a new commit for each picked commit.

commits.description: One or more commit hashes to cherry-pick (space-separated for multiple, e.g. 'abc1234' or 'abc1234 def5678')

no_commit.description: Stage changes without committing — follow with git_commit to finalize (default: false)

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_rebase_continue
tools.description: Continue a rebase after resolving conflicts. Stage resolved files with git_add first, then run this to proceed.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_rebase_abort
tools.description: Abort an in-progress rebase and restore the original branch state.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_cherry_pick_continue
tools.description: Continue a cherry-pick after resolving conflicts. Stage resolved files with git_add first, then run this to proceed.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_cherry_pick_abort
tools.description: Abort an in-progress cherry-pick and restore the original branch state.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

tools.name: git_reset
tools.description: Undo the last commit(s) on the current branch. Defaults to a mixed reset (HEAD~1) which undoes the commit but keeps changes staged — follow with git_commit to re-commit or git_status to inspect.

repo.description: Repository path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix). Defaults to the sandbox root if omitted.

hintConditions.hint: not a git repository — specify a valid repo path

execution.truncationHint: output truncated: {kept} of {total} lines shown; {omitted} more lines available. To paginate, use skip with the same count; use oneline: true for compact output.
output truncated: {kept} of {total} lines shown; {omitted} more lines available. To continue reading, use git_diff_lines with start_line: {next_start}; omit end_line to read the rest.

hintConditions.hint: showing changes since diverging from main

execution.truncationHint: output truncated: {kept} of {total} lines shown; {omitted} more lines available. To continue reading, use git_show_lines with start_line: {next_start}; omit end_line to read the rest.

hintConditions.hint: nothing to commit — working tree clean
unstaged changes present — use git_add to stage them
untracked files present — use git_status to see them
merge conflicts detected — resolve conflicts, then git_add and git_commit
squash merge staged — use git_commit to finalize with a conventional commit message
rebase conflicts detected — resolve conflicts, stage them with git_add, then use git_rebase_continue to proceed (or git_rebase_abort to cancel)
current branch is already up to date with the target
cherry-pick conflicts detected — resolve conflicts, stage them with git_add, then use git_cherry_pick_continue to proceed (or git_cherry_pick_abort to cancel)
cherry-pick staged — use git_commit to finalize with a conventional commit message
reset to {ref} — use git_status to inspect the current state, or git_commit to re-commit staged changes
